fields.description:
Encode the agent-build invariants that guard against silently-disabled safeguards as tests plus a one-time construction check — not as per-call runtime blocking.

## Why
Several dangerous silent failures are static properties of rebar's own agent construction, not per-call events: tools silently stripped when a hook returns None (pydantic-ai #5177), tools silently ignored on a non-tool-calling provider (#6186), `usage_limits` silently ignored if set on the `Agent(...)` constructor instead of `run*` (#1987), and usage silently zeroed on some provider adapters (#5360 cluster). Asserting these per-call would waste work and risk false-blocking a healthy run — they belong in tests + a one-time check.

## What
- Unit/integration tests asserting: the expected tools are actually offered to the agent; `usage_limits` is passed on `run*` (not the constructor); the configured provider supports tool-calling for tool-using ops.
- A one-time capability assertion at model construction (cached, not per-call) that fails fast on a mis-provisioned provider for a tool-using op.
- A usage-plausibility telemetry warning (never a block) when per-provider usage looks zeroed/implausible.

## Scope
- Tests + a construction-time assertion + a telemetry warning. No per-call runtime blocking for these invariants.
- NOT in scope: changing the tool surface or provider adapters themselves.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests fail if the expected tools are not offered / `usage_limits` is mis-placed on the constructor / the provider lacks tool-calling for a tool-using op.
- [ ] A construction-time capability check fails fast (cached, invoked once per model, not per call).
- [ ] Usage-plausibility emits a telemetry warning and never blocks a gate.
- [ ] `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `tests/unit/` (new), `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (construction-time check).

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, offline)
ACTUAL captured output (no billable call):
```
Agent.__init__ has tool_timeout:   True
Agent.__init__ has toolsets/tools:  True   (tools are offered via the constructor; testable introspection)
Agent.run_sync has usage_limits:    True   (#1987: usage_limits belongs on run*, NOT Agent(...) — confirmed
                                            run_sync accepts it, so the mis-placement the test guards is real)
```
The construction-time capability check + the tests assert static invariants (tools actually offered,
usage_limits on run* not the constructor, provider tool-calling) — all against real 1.107.0 API surface.
These are pydantic-ai LIBRARY symbols, not rebar symbols (bug succinct-formable-kite:
a code-grounding pass restricted to the rebar snapshot must not read repo-absence as non-existence).
